reasoning: A positive resolution requires Japan to record more than 10x its baseline 30-day average of protests in a single 30-day period. The prompt notes the baseline is around 166.6. Thus, for a positive resolution, ACLED must record over 1,666 protests in Japan within 30 days (about 55 protests per day). Japan is a politically stable country with no history of experiencing 55 distinct protest events per day for an entire month—such numbers are usually only seen during massive, historic, nationwide upheavals (e.g., peak US Black Lives Matter protests in 2020). For the first two resolution dates (August and September 2025), the 30-day windows are already underway or about to begin, and recent news reports show absolutely no signs of an unprecedented mass protest movement. While uncertainty always naturally increases with time, the fundamental structural likelihood of a 10x protest explosion in Japan remains exceptionally low for the November 2025 and February 2026 windows as well. Thus, the probabilities sit near the system minimum of 0.02 for the first dates and rise only mildly to 0.05 for the furthest date to reflect pure time-horizon uncertainty.
